agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using a region-pinned data sovereignty model with a lightweight global coordination layer. Each region (EU, US, APAC) operates as an autonomous cell owning its customer data, courier assignments, and delivery orchestration. A global control plane aggregates pre-computed, anonymized operational metrics for the dashboard without replicating PII across borders. Region affinity is enforced at the routing layer (GeoDNS + API gateway) and the domain layer (courier-region invariant). Resilience is achieved through intra-region redundancy (multi-AZ) rather than cross-region failover, preserving data residency guarantees.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Global Traffic Router","responsibility":"GeoDNS + Anycast routing that directs customers to their home region's API gateway based on account residency, not IP geolocation. Ensures < 100ms by eliminating cross-region hops.","boundaries":"Stateless. Contains no customer data. Only holds a region-routing table mapping account-id prefixes to regions."},{"name":"Regional Cell (EU / US / APAC)","responsibility":"Fully autonomous unit containing API gateway, application services, domain logic, and regional database. Handles all CRUD for customers, couriers, and deliveries scoped to that region. Each cell can operate independently if other regions are unreachable.","boundaries":"Owns all PII and transactional data for its region. No PII leaves the cell boundary. Inter-cell communication is limited to anonymized metric events."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Authentication, rate limiting, request routing within the cell. Enforces that authenticated users belong to this region; rejects misrouted requests.","boundaries":"Region-local. No cross-region calls on the customer-facing hot path."},{"name":"Delivery Orchestration Service","responsibility":"Manages delivery lifecycle: creation, courier assignment, tracking, completion. Enforces the courier-region invariant at the domain level — a courier's region tag must match the delivery's region.","boundaries":"Region-scoped. Courier pool is strictly regional. No cross-region courier queries."},{"name":"Customer Service","responsibility":"Customer registration, profile management, data subject requests (GDPR/CCPA). Stores all PII in the regional database.","boundaries":"Region-pinned. Data subject deletion/export requests execute entirely within the region."},{"name":"Courier Service","responsibility":"Courier onboarding, availability tracking, location updates. Each courier is tagged with exactly one operating region.","boundaries":"Region-pinned. Courier records never replicate to other regions."},{"name":"Metrics Aggregation Service (Regional)","responsibility":"Produces pre-aggregated, anonymized operational metrics (delivery counts, avg delivery time, SLA compliance rates) on a 5-second tumbling window. Publishes to the global metrics bus.","boundaries":"Emits only aggregate numbers — no PII, no customer IDs, no courier IDs cross the region boundary."},{"name":"Global Metrics Bus","responsibility":"Cross-region event backbone (e.g., AWS EventBridge global endpoints or Kafka MirrorMaker with a dedicated metrics-only topic). Carries only anonymized aggregate metric events between regions.","boundaries":"Strict schema enforcement: only MetricEvent messages allowed. PII fields are structurally absent from the schema (not just filtered — the type doesn't have them)."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard","responsibility":"Reads from the global metrics store to present a unified real-time view across all regions. Hosted in one region but consumes metrics from all three.","boundaries":"Read-only on aggregated data. No write path back to regional systems. No PII access."},{"name":"Global Metrics Store","responsibility":"Time-series database (e.g., InfluxDB/TimescaleDB) storing aggregated metrics from all regions. Powers the dashboard.","boundaries":"Contains only anonymized aggregates. Acceptable to host in a single region since no PII is involved."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Global Traffic Router","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"DNS resolution returns region-local gateway IP. TLS termination at gateway. Account-region affinity header injected.","coupling_type":"loose — DNS-based, no runtime dependency between router and gateway beyond resolution"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Delivery Orchestration Service","contract":"REST/gRPC. CreateDelivery, AssignCourier, GetDeliveryStatus. All requests carry region context in JWT claims.","coupling_type":"tight within region (synchronous), zero coupling across regions"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Customer Service","contract":"REST/gRPC. CRUD operations on customer profiles. GDPR/CCPA data subject request endpoints.","coupling_type":"tight within region"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Courier Service","contract":"REST/gRPC. Courier availability, location updates, assignment confirmations.","coupling_type":"tight within region"},{"from":"Delivery Orchestration Service","to":"Courier Service","contract":"Internal gRPC. QueryAvailableCouriers(region, location, capacity). Region is mandatory and must match delivery region.","coupling_type":"tight within region"},{"from":"Delivery Orchestration Service","to":"Metrics Aggregation Service","contract":"Domain events (DeliveryCreated, DeliveryCompleted, SLABreached) emitted to regional event bus. Metrics service subscribes.","coupling_type":"loose — async event-driven within region"},{"from":"Metrics Aggregation Service","to":"Global Metrics Bus","contract":"MetricEvent schema: {region, metric_name, value, window_start, window_end}. No PII fields in schema. Published every 5 seconds.","coupling_type":"loose — async, fire-and-forget with at-least-once delivery"},{"from":"Global Metrics Bus","to":"Global Metrics Store","contract":"Metrics consumer writes to time-series DB. Idempotent upserts keyed on (region, metric_name, window_start).","coupling_type":"loose"},{"from":"Global Operations Dashboard","to":"Global Metrics Store","contract":"Read-only queries. WebSocket subscription for near-real-time updates (5-10 second refresh).","coupling_type":"loose — read-only"}],"data_flow":"Customer request → GeoDNS resolves to home region → Regional API Gateway authenticates and validates region affinity → Request routed to domain service (Customer/Courier/Delivery) → All reads and writes hit regional database only → Domain events emitted to regional event bus → Metrics Aggregation Service consumes events, computes windowed aggregates → Anonymized MetricEvents published to Global Metrics Bus → Global Metrics Store ingests from all regions → Dashboard queries the store for unified view.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region is an independent cell with its own full stack. This is the only pattern that genuinely guarantees data residency — data physically cannot leave because there is no replication path for PII. It also provides natural blast radius isolation."},{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty by Construction","rationale":"Rather than relying on policy-based controls (which can be misconfigured), the architecture structurally prevents PII from crossing borders. The Global Metrics Bus schema physically lacks PII fields. There is no cross-region database replication for transactional data."},{"pattern":"CQRS for Global Reporting","rationale":"The global dashboard is a read-only projection of anonymized metrics — a separate read model from the regional write models. This decouples operational reporting from transactional processing."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Metrics Aggregation","rationale":"Regional services emit domain events; a dedicated metrics service aggregates them before publishing cross-region. This keeps the hot path (customer requests) free of any cross-region latency."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead / Blast Radius Isolation","rationale":"Region failure is contained within that region's cell. Other regions continue operating. The global dashboard degrades gracefully (shows stale metrics for the downed region)."},{"pattern":"Strangler Fig for Migration","rationale":"Existing EU deployment becomes the EU cell. US and APAC cells are stood up independently. Global routing layer is added on top. No big-bang migration required."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Customer Management","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer (root: CustomerId, contains profile, addresses, preferences, consent records). Invariant: customer belongs to exactly one region, determined at registration and immutable."]},{"name":"Courier Management","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Courier (root: CourierId, contains profile, vehicle info, availability, region tag). Invariant: courier.region is immutable after onboarding. Courier can only appear in queries within their home region."]},{"name":"Delivery Orchestration","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery (root: DeliveryId, contains pickup, dropoff, status, assigned courier, SLA deadlines). Invariant: assignedCourier.region == delivery.region. This is the core domain — competitive advantage comes from optimizing delivery assignment and tracking.","DeliveryRoute (value object: sequence of waypoints, estimated times). Immutable once computed, replaced on re-routing."]},{"name":"Operational Analytics","classification":"generic","aggregates":["RegionalMetricSnapshot (root: region + window_start, contains metric values). No domain logic — pure data aggregation. Generic subdomain; could use off-the-shelf time-series tooling."]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Delivery Orchestration","downstream":"Operational Analytics","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Delivery Orchestration publishes domain events (DeliveryCreated, DeliveryCompleted, SLABreached). Analytics consumes them. Delivery team defines the event schema; Analytics team conforms."},{"upstream":"Courier Management","downstream":"Delivery Orchestration","relationship":"Conformist. Delivery Orchestration queries Courier Management for available couriers. Courier Management owns the courier model; Delivery Orchestration conforms to it and applies its own assignment logic."},{"upstream":"Customer Management","downstream":"Delivery Orchestration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Customer context provides customer details needed for delivery creation. Delivery context receives customer info via an anti-corruption layer to avoid depending on Customer's full model."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional cells (no cross-region PII replication)","gains":"Bulletproof data residency compliance. Simple mental model: region = data boundary. Eliminates entire categories of compliance risk. Each region can be deployed, scaled, and upgraded independently.","sacrifices":"A customer who travels to another region cannot be served from that region's cell — they still hit their home region (with higher latency). No cross-region failover for customer data — if EU goes down, EU customers get degraded service, not a redirect to US.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region replication with encryption-at-rest per region (rejected: encryption doesn't satisfy GDPR data residency — the data still physically exists in another jurisdiction)","Single global database with row-level region tagging (rejected: one misconfigured query leaks data across borders; compliance by policy, not by construction)"]},{"decision":"Anonymized metrics-only global bus (no PII crosses borders)","gains":"Enables the global dashboard without any data residency violations. Simple compliance story for auditors: show the MetricEvent schema — no PII fields exist.","sacrifices":"Global ops team cannot drill down to individual deliveries/customers from the dashboard. They must SSH/VPN into the specific region's tooling for detailed investigation.","alternatives_considered":["Tokenized PII in global bus (rejected: pseudonymization still counts as personal data under GDPR if re-identification is possible)","No global dashboard — each region has its own (rejected: requirement explicitly demands unified real-time view)"]},{"decision":"Intra-region multi-AZ for resilience (not cross-region failover)","gains":"EU customers get degraded-but-functional service during partial EU outages (multi-AZ). Avoids the impossible problem of failing over EU customer data to US while maintaining GDPR compliance.","sacrifices":"Full EU region loss (all AZs) means EU customers are fully offline until region recovers. This is an accepted trade-off given regulatory constraints.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region active-active with GDPR exemption for disaster recovery (rejected: no such blanket exemption exists; would require explicit customer consent and DPA amendments)","EU-West + EU-North dual-region within EU jurisdiction (viable future enhancement — noted in flexibility points)"]},{"decision":"< 100ms via region-local serving, not global CDN for API calls","gains":"All customer API calls resolved within their region. Network latency is intra-region (typically 1-5ms). Leaves 95ms budget for application processing — generous for CRUD and assignment operations.","sacrifices":"Static assets and public content still need a CDN. API responses cannot be cached at edge for personalized data.","alternatives_considered":["Global CDN with edge compute (rejected for dynamic API calls: would require PII at edge nodes, violating residency)","Read replicas at edge (rejected: same residency issue)"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., LATAM, Middle East)","evolution_path":"Cell-based architecture makes this additive. Stand up a new cell, add DNS routing rules, register the region in the global metrics bus. No changes to existing cells required."},{"area":"Intra-jurisdiction multi-region for EU resilience","evolution_path":"EU-West (Ireland) + EU-North (Frankfurt) as two cells within the EU jurisdiction. Both satisfy GDPR. Active-active or active-passive between them gives EU customers true cross-region failover without data residency violations."},{"area":"Global customer accounts (traveling users)","evolution_path":"If business requires it later, implement a 'home region proxy' — a thin edge layer in each region that proxies requests to the customer's home region. Adds latency but preserves data residency. Could cache non-PII data at the local region."},{"area":"Metrics granularity","evolution_path":"Current design aggregates at 5-second windows. Can be tuned to sub-second with Kafka Streams or Flink. The architecture doesn't change — only the window size and compute capacity."},{"area":"Service mesh / sidecar adoption","evolution_path":"Each regional cell can independently adopt a service mesh (Istio/Linkerd) for mTLS, traffic shaping, and observability. Cell independence means no coordinated rollout required."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer-region affinity is immutable","reason":"Once a customer is assigned to a region (at registration), their data lives there permanently. Migrating a customer to another region requires a data portability export + re-registration. This is intentionally rigid — it's the foundation of the compliance guarantee."},{"area":"Courier-region binding","reason":"A courier operates in exactly one region. This is a hard business rule enforced at the domain level. Relaxing it would require a fundamentally different assignment model and potentially cross-region data sharing."},{"area":"No PII in global metrics bus","reason":"The MetricEvent schema structurally excludes PII. This is a load-bearing compliance decision. Any attempt to add PII fields to the schema must go through legal/DPO review. The schema is intentionally locked down."},{"area":"Regional database technology choice","reason":"Each cell's database must be chosen for single-region operation. Multi-region databases (CockroachDB, Spanner) are explicitly not used for transactional data because their replication could violate data residency. This constrains the database to regional options (RDS, Aurora single-region, etc.)."}],"risks":[{"risk":"EU full-region outage causes complete EU customer unavailability","severity":"high","mitigation":"Deploy multi-AZ within the region for partial outage resilience. Plan the EU-West + EU-North dual-cell evolution as a Phase 2 priority. Define RTO/RPO targets and test failover quarterly. Communicate accepted risk to stakeholders."},{"risk":"Global metrics bus becomes a bottleneck or single point of failure for dashboard","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Dashboard is non-critical path — regional operations continue regardless. Use a managed service (EventBridge, Confluent Cloud) with multi-AZ. Dashboard shows 'data unavailable for region X' gracefully when metrics are stale."},{"risk":"Clock skew across regions causes metric window misalignment in global dashboard","severity":"low","mitigation":"Use UTC everywhere. MetricEvent includes window_start and window_end as absolute UTC timestamps. Dashboard aligns on server-side timestamps, not ingestion time."},{"risk":"Misrouted customer request to wrong region exposes data to wrong jurisdiction","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Defense in depth: (1) GeoDNS routes by account residency, (2) API gateway validates region claim in JWT, (3) database connection strings are region-local only — even a misrouted request cannot physically reach another region's data. All three layers must fail simultaneously for a breach."},{"risk":"Schema evolution on the global metrics bus breaks consumers","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use a schema registry (Avro/Protobuf with compatibility checks). MetricEvent schema changes require backward compatibility. Breaking changes require versioned topics and consumer migration."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing three independent cells","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform/Pulumi) with a shared module library. Each cell is an instantiation of the same module with region-specific parameters. CI/CD pipelines deploy to regions independently with canary rollouts. GitOps ensures cell configurations don't drift."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1: Convert existing EU deployment into the EU cell by adding the regional API gateway, region-affinity validation middleware, and metrics aggregation service. Stand up the Global Metrics Bus and Dashboard against EU-only metrics. Phase 2: Provision US cell using IaC modules derived from the EU cell. Configure GeoDNS routing. Validate data isolation with penetration testing and compliance audit. Phase 3: Provision APAC cell. Phase 4: Implement EU intra-jurisdiction dual-cell (EU-West + EU-North) for resilience. Each phase is independently shippable and delivers incremental value.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/modules/regional-cell/ — Terraform module for a complete regional cell (VPC, compute, DB, event bus, services)","infra/environments/eu/ — EU cell configuration parameterizing the regional-cell module","infra/environments/us/ — US cell configuration","infra/environments/apac/ — APAC cell configuration","infra/global/ — Global Traffic Router (Route53/CloudFlare), Global Metrics Bus, Dashboard infrastructure","services/api-gateway/middleware/region-affinity-validator — Middleware that rejects requests where JWT region claim ≠ cell region","services/delivery-orchestration/domain/courier-region-invariant — Domain rule enforcing courier.region == delivery.region at assignment time","services/metrics-aggregation/ — New service: consumes regional domain events, produces anonymized MetricEvents","schemas/metric-event.avro — Avro schema for MetricEvent with no PII fields, registered in schema registry","services/global-dashboard/ — Dashboard application consuming from Global Metrics Store"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture with infrastructure-as-code for cell provisioning. Domain events for intra-region decoupling. Schema registry for cross-region contract enforcement. Defense-in-depth for data residency (DNS + gateway + database isolation). Feature flags per region for phased rollout.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT use a multi-region database (Spanner, CockroachDB, DynamoDB Global Tables) for customer/courier/delivery data — replication violates data residency by design","Do NOT rely solely on application-level checks for data residency — network-level isolation (separate VPCs, no peering for data stores) is essential","Do NOT put PII in logs shipped to a central logging platform — either keep logs regional or strip PII before centralization","Do NOT assume IP geolocation equals customer region — a traveling EU customer in the US must still be routed to the EU cell","Do NOT build cross-region API calls on the customer hot path — every synchronous cross-region call adds 80-200ms latency and creates a region coupling point","Do NOT share database credentials or connection strings across cells — each cell's secrets are scoped to its region only"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Cell-based isolation is the correct pattern for data residency — this is well-established in regulated multi-region systems","Anonymized metrics bus for global dashboard — standard approach that avoids all PII transfer issues","Courier-region invariant as a domain-level enforcement — clean DDD aggregate boundary","Defense-in-depth data residency (DNS + gateway + DB isolation) — industry best practice","Phased migration starting from existing EU deployment — low-risk incremental approach"],"low_confidence_areas":["Specific latency budget allocation within the 100ms target — would need load testing to validate that intra-region processing stays under 95ms for P99","APAC data residency requirements — GDPR and CCPA are specified but APAC regulations (e.g., China's PIPL, Japan's APPI) may impose additional constraints not captured here","Global dashboard refresh rate (5-second windows) — may or may not meet the 'real time' expectation; needs stakeholder alignment on what 'real time' means"],"assumptions":["APAC does not currently have data residency requirements as strict as GDPR/CCPA (if it does, the same cell model applies but may need sub-region cells, e.g., China-specific)","The existing EU system is a monolith or set of services that can be wrapped with the regional gateway pattern without major refactoring","'Degraded but functional service' for EU region failure means read-from-cache/stale-data acceptable, not full write capability during outage","The global operations dashboard is used by internal staff only, not customers, so slightly higher latency (seconds) is acceptable","Each region will have sufficient courier density — the architecture does not solve the cold-start problem of launching in a new region with few couriers"]}}

Let me analyze this multi-region logistics platform architecture problem carefully.

Classification: `none` — sufficient constraints exist. We have clear NFRs (data residency, latency, availability), scale requirements (3 regions), and integration boundaries (cross-region metrics, courier-region binding).

Let me design this.

content.text: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using a region-pinned data sovereignty model with a lightweight global coordination layer. Each region (EU, US, APAC) operates as an autonomous cell owning its customer data, courier assignments, and delivery orchestration. A global control plane aggregates pre-computed, anonymized operational metrics for the dashboard without replicating PII across borders. Region affinity is enforced at the routing layer (GeoDNS + API gateway) and the domain layer (courier-region invariant). Resilience is achieved through intra-region redundancy (multi-AZ) rather than cross-region failover, preserving data residency guarantees.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Global Traffic Router","responsibility":"GeoDNS + Anycast routing that directs customers to their home region's API gateway based on account residency, not IP geolocation. Ensures < 100ms by eliminating cross-region hops.","boundaries":"Stateless. Contains no customer data. Only holds a region-routing table mapping account-id prefixes to regions."},{"name":"Regional Cell (EU / US / APAC)","responsibility":"Fully autonomous unit containing API gateway, application services, domain logic, and regional database. Handles all CRUD for customers, couriers, and deliveries scoped to that region. Each cell can operate independently if other regions are unreachable.","boundaries":"Owns all PII and transactional data for its region. No PII leaves the cell boundary. Inter-cell communication is limited to anonymized metric events."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Authentication, rate limiting, request routing within the cell. Enforces that authenticated users belong to this region; rejects misrouted requests.","boundaries":"Region-local. No cross-region calls on the customer-facing hot path."},{"name":"Delivery Orchestration Service","responsibility":"Manages delivery lifecycle: creation, courier assignment, tracking, completion. No cross-region failover for customer data — if EU goes down, EU customers get degraded service, not a redirect to US.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region replication with encryption-at-rest per region (rejected: encryption doesn't satisfy GDPR data residency — the data still physically exists in another jurisdiction)","Single global database with row-level region tagging (rejected: one misconfigured query leaks data across borders; compliance by policy, not by construction)"]},{"decision":"Anonymized metrics-only global bus (no PII crosses borders)","gains":"Enables the global dashboard without any data residency violations. Simple compliance story for auditors: show the MetricEvent schema — no PII fields exist.","sacrifices":"Global ops team cannot drill down to individual deliveries/customers from the dashboard. They must SSH/VPN into the specific region's tooling for detailed investigation.","alternatives_considered":["Tokenized PII in global bus (rejected: pseudonymization still counts as personal data under GDPR if re-identification is possible)","No global dashboard — each region has its own (rejected: requirement explicitly demands unified real-time view)"]},{"decision":"Intra-region multi-AZ for resilience (not cross-region failover)","gains":"EU customers get degraded-but-functional service during partial EU outages (multi-AZ). Avoids the impossible problem of failing over EU customer data to US while maintaining GDPR compliance.","sacrifices":"Full EU region loss (all AZs) means EU customers are fully offline until region recovers. This is an accepted trade-off given regulatory constraints.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region active-active with GDPR exemption for disaster recovery (rejected: no such blanket exemption exists; would require explicit customer consent and DPA amendments)","EU-West + EU-North dual-region within EU jurisdiction (viable future enhancement — noted in flexibility points)"]},{"decision":"< 100ms via region-local serving, not global CDN for API calls","gains":"All customer API calls resolved within their region. Network latency is intra-region (typically 1-5ms). Leaves 95ms budget for application processing — generous for CRUD and assignment operations.","sacrifices":"Static assets and public content still need a CDN. API responses cannot be cached at edge for personalized data.","alternatives_considered":["Global CDN with edge compute (rejected for dynamic API calls: would require PII at edge nodes, violating residency)","Read replicas at edge (rejected: same residency issue)"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., LATAM, Middle East)","evolution_path":"Cell-based architecture makes this additive. Stand up a new cell, add DNS routing rules, register the region in the global metrics bus. No changes to existing cells required."},{"area":"Intra-jurisdiction multi-region for EU resilience","evolution_path":"EU-West (Ireland) + EU-North (Frankfurt) as two cells within the EU jurisdiction. Both satisfy GDPR. Active-active or active-passive between them gives EU customers true cross-region failover without data residency violations."},{"area":"Global customer accounts (traveling users)","evolution_path":"If business requires it later, implement a 'home region proxy' — a thin edge layer in each region that proxies requests to the customer's home region. Adds latency but preserves data residency. Could cache non-PII data at the local region."},{"area":"Metrics granularity","evolution_path":"Current design aggregates at 5-second windows. Can be tuned to sub-second with Kafka Streams or Flink. The architecture doesn't change — only the window size and compute capacity."},{"area":"Service mesh / sidecar adoption","evolution_path":"Each regional cell can independently adopt a service mesh (Istio/Linkerd) for mTLS, traffic shaping, and observability. Cell independence means no coordinated rollout required."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer-region affinity is immutable","reason":"Once a customer is assigned to a region (at registration), their data lives there permanently. Migrating a customer to another region requires a data portability export + re-registration. This is intentionally rigid — it's the foundation of the compliance guarantee."},{"area":"Courier-region binding","reason":"A courier operates in exactly one region. This is a hard business rule enforced at the domain level. Relaxing it would require a fundamentally different assignment model and potentially cross-region data sharing."},{"area":"No PII in global metrics bus","reason":"The MetricEvent schema structurally excludes PII. This is a load-bearing compliance decision. Any attempt to add PII fields to the schema must go through legal/DPO review. The schema is intentionally locked down."},{"area":"Regional database technology choice","reason":"Each cell's database must be chosen for single-region operation. Multi-region databases (CockroachDB, Spanner) are explicitly not used for transactional data because their replication could violate data residency. This constrains the database to regional options (RDS, Aurora single-region, etc.)."}],"risks":[{"risk":"EU full-region outage causes complete EU customer unavailability","severity":"high","mitigation":"Deploy multi-AZ within the region for partial outage resilience. Plan the EU-West + EU-North dual-cell evolution as a Phase 2 priority. Define RTO/RPO targets and test failover quarterly. Communicate accepted risk to stakeholders."},{"risk":"Global metrics bus becomes a bottleneck or single point of failure for dashboard","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Dashboard is non-critical path — regional operations continue regardless. Use a managed service (EventBridge, Confluent Cloud) with multi-AZ. Dashboard shows 'data unavailable for region X' gracefully when metrics are stale."},{"risk":"Clock skew across regions causes metric window misalignment in global dashboard","severity":"low","mitigation":"Use UTC everywhere. MetricEvent includes window_start and window_end as absolute UTC timestamps. Dashboard aligns on server-side timestamps, not ingestion time."},{"risk":"Misrouted customer request to wrong region exposes data to wrong jurisdiction","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Defense in depth: (1) GeoDNS routes by account residency, (2) API gateway validates region claim in JWT, (3) database connection strings are region-local only — even a misrouted request cannot physically reach another region's data. All three layers must fail simultaneously for a breach."},{"risk":"Schema evolution on the global metrics bus breaks consumers","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use a schema registry (Avro/Protobuf with compatibility checks). MetricEvent schema changes require backward compatibility. Breaking changes require versioned topics and consumer migration."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing three independent cells","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform/Pulumi) with a shared module library. Each cell is an instantiation of the same module with region-specific parameters. CI/CD pipelines deploy to regions independently with canary rollouts. GitOps ensures cell configurations don't drift."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1: Convert existing EU deployment into the EU cell by adding the regional API gateway, region-affinity validation middleware, and metrics aggregation service. Stand up the Global Metrics Bus and Dashboard against EU-only metrics. Phase 2: Provision US cell using IaC modules derived from the EU cell. Configure GeoDNS routing. Validate data isolation with penetration testing and compliance audit. Phase 3: Provision APAC cell. Phase 4: Implement EU intra-jurisdiction dual-cell (EU-West + EU-North) for resilience. Each phase is independently shippable and delivers incremental value.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/modules/regional-cell/ — Terraform module for a complete regional cell (VPC, compute, DB, event bus, services)","infra/environments/eu/ — EU cell configuration parameterizing the regional-cell module","infra/environments/us/ — US cell configuration","infra/environments/apac/ — APAC cell configuration","infra/global/ — Global Traffic Router (Route53/CloudFlare), Global Metrics Bus, Dashboard infrastructure","services/api-gateway/middleware/region-affinity-validator — Middleware that rejects requests where JWT region claim ≠ cell region","services/delivery-orchestration/domain/courier-region-invariant — Domain rule enforcing courier.region == delivery.region at assignment time","services/metrics-aggregation/ — New service: consumes regional domain events, produces anonymized MetricEvents","schemas/metric-event.avro — Avro schema for MetricEvent with no PII fields, registered in schema registry","services/global-dashboard/ — Dashboard application consuming from Global Metrics Store"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture with infrastructure-as-code for cell provisioning. Domain events for intra-region decoupling. Schema registry for cross-region contract enforcement. Defense-in-depth for data residency (DNS + gateway + database isolation). Feature flags per region for phased rollout.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT use a multi-region database (Spanner, CockroachDB, DynamoDB Global Tables) for customer/courier/delivery data — replication violates data residency by design","Do NOT rely solely on application-level checks for data residency — network-level isolation (separate VPCs, no peering for data stores) is essential","Do NOT put PII in logs shipped to a central logging platform — either keep logs regional or strip PII before centralization","Do NOT assume IP geolocation equals customer region — a traveling EU customer in the US must still be routed to the EU cell","Do NOT build cross-region API calls on the customer hot path — every synchronous cross-region call adds 80-200ms latency and creates a region coupling point","Do NOT share database credentials or connection strings across cells — each cell's secrets are scoped to its region only"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Cell-based isolation is the correct pattern for data residency — this is well-established in regulated multi-region systems","Anonymized metrics bus for global dashboard — standard approach that avoids all PII transfer issues","Courier-region invariant as a domain-level enforcement — clean DDD aggregate boundary","Defense-in-depth data residency (DNS + gateway + DB isolation) — industry best practice","Phased migration starting from existing EU deployment — low-risk incremental approach"],"low_confidence_areas":["Specific latency budget allocation within the 100ms target — would need load testing to validate that intra-region processing stays under 95ms for P99","APAC data residency requirements — GDPR and CCPA are specified but APAC regulations (e.g., China's PIPL, Japan's APPI) may impose additional constraints not captured here","Global dashboard refresh rate (5-second windows) — may or may not meet the 'real time' expectation; needs stakeholder alignment on what 'real time' means"],"assumptions":["APAC does not currently have data residency requirements as strict as GDPR/CCPA (if it does, the same cell model applies but may need sub-region cells, e.g., China-specific)","The existing EU system is a monolith or set of services that can be wrapped with the regional gateway pattern without major refactoring","'Degraded but functional service' for EU region failure means read-from-cache/stale-data acceptable, not full write capability during outage","The global operations dashboard is used by internal staff only, not customers, so slightly higher latency (seconds) is acceptable","Each region will have sufficient courier density — the architecture does not solve the cold-start problem of launching in a new region with few couriers"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using a region-pinned data sovereignty model with a lightweight global coordination layer. Each region (EU, US, APAC) operates as an autonomous cell owning its customer data, courier assignments, and delivery orchestration. A global control plane aggregates pre-computed, anonymized operational metrics for the dashboard without replicating PII across borders. Region affinity is enforced at the routing layer (GeoDNS + API gateway) and the domain layer (courier-region invariant). Resilience is achieved through intra-region redundancy (multi-AZ) rather than cross-region failover, preserving data residency guarantees.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Global Traffic Router","responsibility":"GeoDNS + Anycast routing that directs customers to their home region's API gateway based on account residency, not IP geolocation. Ensures < 100ms by eliminating cross-region hops.","boundaries":"Stateless. Contains no customer data. Only holds a region-routing table mapping account-id prefixes to regions."},{"name":"Regional Cell (EU / US / APAC)","responsibility":"Fully autonomous unit containing API gateway, application services, domain logic, and regional database. Handles all CRUD for customers, couriers, and deliveries scoped to that region. Each cell can operate independently if other regions are unreachable.","boundaries":"Owns all PII and transactional data for its region. No PII leaves the cell boundary. Inter-cell communication is limited to anonymized metric events."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Authentication, rate limiting, request routing within the cell. Enforces that authenticated users belong to this region; rejects misrouted requests.","boundaries":"Region-local. No cross-region calls on the customer-facing hot path."},{"name":"Delivery Orchestration Service","responsibility":"Manages delivery lifecycle: creation, courier assignment, tracking, completion. Enforces the courier-region invariant at the domain level — a courier's region tag must match the delivery's region.","boundaries":"Region-scoped. Courier pool is strictly regional. No cross-region courier queries."},{"name":"Customer Service","responsibility":"Customer registration, profile management, data subject requests (GDPR/CCPA). Stores all PII in the regional database.","boundaries":"Region-pinned. Data subject deletion/export requests execute entirely within the region."},{"name":"Courier Service","responsibility":"Courier onboarding, availability tracking, location updates. Each courier is tagged with exactly one operating region.","boundaries":"Region-pinned. Courier records never replicate to other regions."},{"name":"Metrics Aggregation Service (Regional)","responsibility":"Produces pre-aggregated, anonymized operational metrics (delivery counts, avg delivery time, SLA compliance rates) on a 5-second tumbling window. Publishes to the global metrics bus.","boundaries":"Emits only aggregate numbers — no PII, no customer IDs, no courier IDs cross the region boundary."},{"name":"Global Metrics Bus","responsibility":"Cross-region event backbone (e.g., AWS EventBridge global endpoints or Kafka MirrorMaker with a dedicated metrics-only topic). Carries only anonymized aggregate metric events between regions.","boundaries":"Strict schema enforcement: only MetricEvent messages allowed. PII fields are structurally absent from the schema (not just filtered — the type doesn't have them)."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard","responsibility":"Reads from the global metrics store to present a unified real-time view across all regions. Hosted in one region but consumes metrics from all three.","boundaries":"Read-only on aggregated data. No write path back to regional systems. No PII access."},{"name":"Global Metrics Store","responsibility":"Time-series database (e.g., InfluxDB/TimescaleDB) storing aggregated metrics from all regions. Powers the dashboard.","boundaries":"Contains only anonymized aggregates. Acceptable to host in a single region since no PII is involved."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Global Traffic Router","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"DNS resolution returns region-local gateway IP. TLS termination at gateway. Account-region affinity header injected.","coupling_type":"loose — DNS-based, no runtime dependency between router and gateway beyond resolution"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Delivery Orchestration Service","contract":"REST/gRPC. CreateDelivery, AssignCourier, GetDeliveryStatus. All requests carry region context in JWT claims.","coupling_type":"tight within region (synchronous), zero coupling across regions"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Customer Service","contract":"REST/gRPC. CRUD operations on customer profiles. GDPR/CCPA data subject request endpoints.","coupling_type":"tight within region"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Courier Service","contract":"REST/gRPC. Courier availability, location updates, assignment confirmations.","coupling_type":"tight within region"},{"from":"Delivery Orchestration Service","to":"Courier Service","contract":"Internal gRPC. QueryAvailableCouriers(region, location, capacity). Region is mandatory and must match delivery region.","coupling_type":"tight within region"},{"from":"Delivery Orchestration Service","to":"Metrics Aggregation Service","contract":"Domain events (DeliveryCreated, DeliveryCompleted, SLABreached) emitted to regional event bus. Metrics service subscribes.","coupling_type":"loose — async event-driven within region"},{"from":"Metrics Aggregation Service","to":"Global Metrics Bus","contract":"MetricEvent schema: {region, metric_name, value, window_start, window_end}. No PII fields in schema. Published every 5 seconds.","coupling_type":"loose — async, fire-and-forget with at-least-once delivery"},{"from":"Global Metrics Bus","to":"Global Metrics Store","contract":"Metrics consumer writes to time-series DB. Idempotent upserts keyed on (region, metric_name, window_start).","coupling_type":"loose"},{"from":"Global Operations Dashboard","to":"Global Metrics Store","contract":"Read-only queries. WebSocket subscription for near-real-time updates (5-10 second refresh).","coupling_type":"loose — read-only"}],"data_flow":"Customer request → GeoDNS resolves to home region → Regional API Gateway authenticates and validates region affinity → Request routed to domain service (Customer/Courier/Delivery) → All reads and writes hit regional database only → Domain events emitted to regional event bus → Metrics Aggregation Service consumes events, computes windowed aggregates → Anonymized MetricEvents published to Global Metrics Bus → Global Metrics Store ingests from all regions → Dashboard queries the store for unified view.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region is an independent cell with its own full stack. This is the only pattern that genuinely guarantees data residency — data physically cannot leave because there is no replication path for PII. It also provides natural blast radius isolation."},{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty by Construction","rationale":"Rather than relying on policy-based controls (which can be misconfigured), the architecture structurally prevents PII from crossing borders. The Global Metrics Bus schema physically lacks PII fields. There is no cross-region database replication for transactional data."},{"pattern":"CQRS for Global Reporting","rationale":"The global dashboard is a read-only projection of anonymized metrics — a separate read model from the regional write models. This decouples operational reporting from transactional processing."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Metrics Aggregation","rationale":"Regional services emit domain events; a dedicated metrics service aggregates them before publishing cross-region. This keeps the hot path (customer requests) free of any cross-region latency."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead / Blast Radius Isolation","rationale":"Region failure is contained within that region's cell. Other regions continue operating. The global dashboard degrades gracefully (shows stale metrics for the downed region)."},{"pattern":"Strangler Fig for Migration","rationale":"Existing EU deployment becomes the EU cell. US and APAC cells are stood up independently. Global routing layer is added on top. No big-bang migration required."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Customer Management","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer (root: CustomerId, contains profile, addresses, preferences, consent records). Invariant: customer belongs to exactly one region, determined at registration and immutable."]},{"name":"Courier Management","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Courier (root: CourierId, contains profile, vehicle info, availability, region tag). Invariant: courier.region is immutable after onboarding. Courier can only appear in queries within their home region."]},{"name":"Delivery Orchestration","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery (root: DeliveryId, contains pickup, dropoff, status, assigned courier, SLA deadlines). Invariant: assignedCourier.region == delivery.region. This is the core domain — competitive advantage comes from optimizing delivery assignment and tracking.","DeliveryRoute (value object: sequence of waypoints, estimated times). Immutable once computed, replaced on re-routing."]},{"name":"Operational Analytics","classification":"generic","aggregates":["RegionalMetricSnapshot (root: region + window_start, contains metric values). No domain logic — pure data aggregation. Generic subdomain; could use off-the-shelf time-series tooling."]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Delivery Orchestration","downstream":"Operational Analytics","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Delivery Orchestration publishes domain events (DeliveryCreated, DeliveryCompleted, SLABreached). Analytics consumes them. Delivery team defines the event schema; Analytics team conforms."},{"upstream":"Courier Management","downstream":"Delivery Orchestration","relationship":"Conformist. Delivery Orchestration queries Courier Management for available couriers. Courier Management owns the courier model; Delivery Orchestration conforms to it and applies its own assignment logic."},{"upstream":"Customer Management","downstream":"Delivery Orchestration","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Customer context provides customer details needed for delivery creation. Delivery context receives customer info via an anti-corruption layer to avoid depending on Customer's full model."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional cells (no cross-region PII replication)","gains":"Bulletproof data residency compliance. Simple mental model: region = data boundary. Eliminates entire categories of compliance risk. Each region can be deployed, scaled, and upgraded independently.","sacrifices":"A customer who travels to another region cannot be served from that region's cell — they still hit their home region (with higher latency). No cross-region failover for customer data — if EU goes down, EU customers get degraded service, not a redirect to US.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region replication with encryption-at-rest per region (rejected: encryption doesn't satisfy GDPR data residency — the data still physically exists in another jurisdiction)","Single global database with row-level region tagging (rejected: one misconfigured query leaks data across borders; compliance by policy, not by construction)"]},{"decision":"Anonymized metrics-only global bus (no PII crosses borders)","gains":"Enables the global dashboard without any data residency violations. Simple compliance story for auditors: show the MetricEvent schema — no PII fields exist.","sacrifices":"Global ops team cannot drill down to individual deliveries/customers from the dashboard. They must SSH/VPN into the specific region's tooling for detailed investigation.","alternatives_considered":["Tokenized PII in global bus (rejected: pseudonymization still counts as personal data under GDPR if re-identification is possible)","No global dashboard — each region has its own (rejected: requirement explicitly demands unified real-time view)"]},{"decision":"Intra-region multi-AZ for resilience (not cross-region failover)","gains":"EU customers get degraded-but-functional service during partial EU outages (multi-AZ). Avoids the impossible problem of failing over EU customer data to US while maintaining GDPR compliance.","sacrifices":"Full EU region loss (all AZs) means EU customers are fully offline until region recovers. This is an accepted trade-off given regulatory constraints.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region active-active with GDPR exemption for disaster recovery (rejected: no such blanket exemption exists; would require explicit customer consent and DPA amendments)","EU-West + EU-North dual-region within EU jurisdiction (viable future enhancement — noted in flexibility points)"]},{"decision":"< 100ms via region-local serving, not global CDN for API calls","gains":"All customer API calls resolved within their region. Network latency is intra-region (typically 1-5ms). Leaves 95ms budget for application processing — generous for CRUD and assignment operations.","sacrifices":"Static assets and public content still need a CDN. API responses cannot be cached at edge for personalized data.","alternatives_considered":["Global CDN with edge compute (rejected for dynamic API calls: would require PII at edge nodes, violating residency)","Read replicas at edge (rejected: same residency issue)"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., LATAM, Middle East)","evolution_path":"Cell-based architecture makes this additive. Stand up a new cell, add DNS routing rules, register the region in the global metrics bus. No changes to existing cells required."},{"area":"Intra-jurisdiction multi-region for EU resilience","evolution_path":"EU-West (Ireland) + EU-North (Frankfurt) as two cells within the EU jurisdiction. Both satisfy GDPR. Active-active or active-passive between them gives EU customers true cross-region failover without data residency violations."},{"area":"Global customer accounts (traveling users)","evolution_path":"If business requires it later, implement a 'home region proxy' — a thin edge layer in each region that proxies requests to the customer's home region. Adds latency but preserves data residency. Could cache non-PII data at the local region."},{"area":"Metrics granularity","evolution_path":"Current design aggregates at 5-second windows. Can be tuned to sub-second with Kafka Streams or Flink. The architecture doesn't change — only the window size and compute capacity."},{"area":"Service mesh / sidecar adoption","evolution_path":"Each regional cell can independently adopt a service mesh (Istio/Linkerd) for mTLS, traffic shaping, and observability. Cell independence means no coordinated rollout required."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer-region affinity is immutable","reason":"Once a customer is assigned to a region (at registration), their data lives there permanently. Migrating a customer to another region requires a data portability export + re-registration. This is intentionally rigid — it's the foundation of the compliance guarantee."},{"area":"Courier-region binding","reason":"A courier operates in exactly one region. This is a hard business rule enforced at the domain level. Relaxing it would require a fundamentally different assignment model and potentially cross-region data sharing."},{"area":"No PII in global metrics bus","reason":"The MetricEvent schema structurally excludes PII. This is a load-bearing compliance decision. Any attempt to add PII fields to the schema must go through legal/DPO review. The schema is intentionally locked down."},{"area":"Regional database technology choice","reason":"Each cell's database must be chosen for single-region operation. Multi-region databases (CockroachDB, Spanner) are explicitly not used for transactional data because their replication could violate data residency. This constrains the database to regional options (RDS, Aurora single-region, etc.)."}],"risks":[{"risk":"EU full-region outage causes complete EU customer unavailability","severity":"high","mitigation":"Deploy multi-AZ within the region for partial outage resilience. Plan the EU-West + EU-North dual-cell evolution as a Phase 2 priority. Define RTO/RPO targets and test failover quarterly. Communicate accepted risk to stakeholders."},{"risk":"Global metrics bus becomes a bottleneck or single point of failure for dashboard","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Dashboard is non-critical path — regional operations continue regardless. Use a managed service (EventBridge, Confluent Cloud) with multi-AZ. Dashboard shows 'data unavailable for region X' gracefully when metrics are stale."},{"risk":"Clock skew across regions causes metric window misalignment in global dashboard","severity":"low","mitigation":"Use UTC everywhere. MetricEvent includes window_start and window_end as absolute UTC timestamps. Dashboard aligns on server-side timestamps, not ingestion time."},{"risk":"Misrouted customer request to wrong region exposes data to wrong jurisdiction","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Defense in depth: (1) GeoDNS routes by account residency, (2) API gateway validates region claim in JWT, (3) database connection strings are region-local only — even a misrouted request cannot physically reach another region's data. All three layers must fail simultaneously for a breach."},{"risk":"Schema evolution on the global metrics bus breaks consumers","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use a schema registry (Avro/Protobuf with compatibility checks). MetricEvent schema changes require backward compatibility. Breaking changes require versioned topics and consumer migration."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing three independent cells","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform/Pulumi) with a shared module library. Each cell is an instantiation of the same module with region-specific parameters. CI/CD pipelines deploy to regions independently with canary rollouts. GitOps ensures cell configurations don't drift."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase 1: Convert existing EU deployment into the EU cell by adding the regional API gateway, region-affinity validation middleware, and metrics aggregation service. Stand up the Global Metrics Bus and Dashboard against EU-only metrics. Phase 2: Provision US cell using IaC modules derived from the EU cell. Configure GeoDNS routing. Validate data isolation with penetration testing and compliance audit. Phase 3: Provision APAC cell. Phase 4: Implement EU intra-jurisdiction dual-cell (EU-West + EU-North) for resilience. Each phase is independently shippable and delivers incremental value.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/modules/regional-cell/ — Terraform module for a complete regional cell (VPC, compute, DB, event bus, services)","infra/environments/eu/ — EU cell configuration parameterizing the regional-cell module","infra/environments/us/ — US cell configuration","infra/environments/apac/ — APAC cell configuration","infra/global/ — Global Traffic Router (Route53/CloudFlare), Global Metrics Bus, Dashboard infrastructure","services/api-gateway/middleware/region-affinity-validator — Middleware that rejects requests where JWT region claim ≠ cell region","services/delivery-orchestration/domain/courier-region-invariant — Domain rule enforcing courier.region == delivery.region at assignment time","services/metrics-aggregation/ — New service: consumes regional domain events, produces anonymized MetricEvents","schemas/metric-event.avro — Avro schema for MetricEvent with no PII fields, registered in schema registry","services/global-dashboard/ — Dashboard application consuming from Global Metrics Store"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture with infrastructure-as-code for cell provisioning. Domain events for intra-region decoupling. Schema registry for cross-region contract enforcement. Defense-in-depth for data residency (DNS + gateway + database isolation). Feature flags per region for phased rollout.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT use a multi-region database (Spanner, CockroachDB, DynamoDB Global Tables) for customer/courier/delivery data — replication violates data residency by design","Do NOT rely solely on application-level checks for data residency — network-level isolation (separate VPCs, no peering for data stores) is essential","Do NOT put PII in logs shipped to a central logging platform — either keep logs regional or strip PII before centralization","Do NOT assume IP geolocation equals customer region — a traveling EU customer in the US must still be routed to the EU cell","Do NOT build cross-region API calls on the customer hot path — every synchronous cross-region call adds 80-200ms latency and creates a region coupling point","Do NOT share database credentials or connection strings across cells — each cell's secrets are scoped to its region only"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Cell-based isolation is the correct pattern for data residency — this is well-established in regulated multi-region systems","Anonymized metrics bus for global dashboard — standard approach that avoids all PII transfer issues","Courier-region invariant as a domain-level enforcement — clean DDD aggregate boundary","Defense-in-depth data residency (DNS + gateway + DB isolation) — industry best practice","Phased migration starting from existing EU deployment — low-risk incremental approach"],"low_confidence_areas":["Specific latency budget allocation within the 100ms target — would need load testing to validate that intra-region processing stays under 95ms for P99","APAC data residency requirements — GDPR and CCPA are specified but APAC regulations (e.g., China's PIPL, Japan's APPI) may impose additional constraints not captured here","Global dashboard refresh rate (5-second windows) — may or may not meet the 'real time' expectation;
